description_en: Belonging to the very distant past and no longer in existence.
example: The ruins of the ancient city are a popular tourist destination.
note: Refers to thousands of years ago.

description_en: An object made by a human being, typically one of cultural or historical interest.
example: The museum displays many artifacts from the Mayan civilization.

description_en: The stage of human social and cultural development and organization that is considered Most advanced.
example: The Indus Valley civilization was one of the earliest urban societies.
note: Complex society with cities and government.

description_en: A line of hereditary rulers of a country.
example: The Ming Dynasty ruled China for nearly 300 years.
note: Succession of rulers from one family.

description_en: An extensive group of states or countries under a single supreme authority.
example: The Roman Empire stretched across Europe, Africa, and Asia.
note: Ruled by an emperor or empress.

description_en: The dominant social system in medieval Europe.
example: Feudalism was based on the holding of land in exchange for service or labor.

description_en: A line of descent traced continuously from an ancestor.
example: She has spent years researching her family's genealogy.

description_en: A system or organization in which people or groups are ranked one above the other according to status or authority.
example: The social hierarchy of ancient Egypt was very rigid.

description_en: The action of inheriting property, rank, or title.
example: The king's eldest son was the primary heir to the inheritance.

description_en: Just behavior or treatment.
example: The code of laws was designed to ensure justice for all citizens.

description_en: A country, state, or territory ruled by a king or queen.
example: The kingdom was at peace during his long reign.

description_en: An amount of money or property left to someone in a will.
example: The Greeks left a lasting legacy of democracy and philosophy.

description_en: A statue, building, or other structure erected to commemorate a notable person or event.
example: The Great Wall is a massive monument to China's history.

description_en: The quality of being noble in character, mind, birth, or rank.
example: Members of the nobility often held high positions in government.

meaning: chế độ tài phiệt; thiểu số trị
description_en: A small group of people having control of a country, organization, or institution.
example: Ancient Sparta was often described as an oligarchy.
note: Rule by a small elite group.

description_en: Relating to or denoting the period before written records.
example: Cave paintings provide a window into prehistoric human life.

description_en: The female ruler of an independent state, especially one who inherits the position by right of birth.
example: Queen Victoria ruled for more than 63 years.

description_en: The revival of art and literature under the influence of classical models in the 14th–16th centuries.
example: The Renaissance began in Italy and spread throughout Europe.
note: A period of great cultural growth.

description_en: Supreme power or authority.
example: The declaration affirmed the nation's independence and sovereignty.

description_en: The transmission of customs or beliefs from generation to generation.
example: The family still follows many ancient cultural traditions.

description_en: The process of being united or made into a whole.
example: The unification of the diverse tribes took several decades.

description_en: A holder of land by feudal tenure on conditions of homage and allegiance.
example: The local lord was a direct vassal of the king.

description_en: Engagement in or the activities of waging war.
example: Siege warfare was a common tactic in medieval times.

description_en: Dislike of or prejudice against people from other countries.
example: History shows that economic crises can often trigger waves of xenophobia.
note: Often leads to conflict between civilizations.

description_en: Give way to arguments, demands, or pressure.
example: The besieged city refused to yield to the invading army.

description_en: A rectangular stepped tower, sometimes surmounted by a temple.
example: The Great Ziggurat of Ur is one of the Most famous ancient structures.

description_en: The study of human history and prehistory through the excavation of sites and the analysis of artifacts and other physical remains.
example: Archaeology helps us reconstruct the daily lives of ancient people.

meaning: người man tộc; kẻ dã man
description_en: A member of a community or tribe not belonging to one of the great civilizations.
example: The Greeks called anyone who didn't speak their language a barbarian.
note: Often used as a biased term for outsiders.

description_en: A factual written account of important or historical events in the order of their occurrence.
example: The Anglo-Saxon Chronicle is a major source for early English history.

description_en: A system of government by the whole population or all the eligible members of a state, typically through elected representatives.
example: Democracy had its origins in the city-state of Athens.

description_en: A long and distinct period of history with a particular feature or characteristic.
example: The Victorian era was a time of great social and economic change.

meaning: công trình phòng thủ; sự kiên cố hóa
description_en: A defensive wall or other reinforcement built to strengthen a place against attack.
example: The city was protected by a series of massive stone fortifications.

description_en: A man trained to fight with weapons against other men or wild animals in an arena.
example: Gladiators were often slaves or prisoners of war in ancient Rome.

description_en: Property that is or may be inherited; an inheritance.
example: The site is recognized as part of the world's cultural heritage.

description_en: Words inscribed, as on a monument or in a book.
example: The stone tablet bears an ancient inscription in a lost language.
note: Written text on a hard surface.

description_en: The collections of laws and legal interpretations developed under the Byzantine emperor Justinian I.
example: The Justinian Code formed the basis for much of modern civil law.

description_en: A furnace or oven for burning, baking, or drying.
example: Archaeologists found remains of a large pottery kiln at the site.
note: Used for manufacturing brick and pottery.

description_en: Direct descent from an ancestor; ancestry or pedigree.
example: The prince was proud of his ancient and distinguished lineage.

description_en: Relating to the Middle Ages.
example: The castle is a well-preserved example of medieval architecture.
note: Refers to the period between 500-1500 AD.

description_en: A member of a people having no permanent abode, and who travel from place to place to find fresh pasture for their livestock.
example: The Bedouin are a well-known group of desert nomads.

description_en: The collection and study of historical information using sound recordings of interviews with people having personal knowledge of past events.
example: Oral history is vital for preserving the traditions of indigenous peoples.
note: History passed down by word of mouth.

description_en: A ruler in ancient Egypt.
example: Tutankhamun is one of the Most famous pharaohs in history.

description_en: A particular district of a city or town.
example: The Jewish quarter of the old city has been carefully restored.

description_en: An object surviving from an earlier time, especially one of historical or sentimental interest.
example: The ancient weapon is a rare relic from the Iron Age.

meaning: người chép thuê; viên lục sự
description_en: A person who copies out documents, especially one employed to do this before printing was invented.
example: In ancient times, scribes were respected for their ability to read and write.

description_en: A building devoted to the worship, or regarded as the dwelling place, of a god or gods or other objects of religious reverence.
example: The temple was dedicated to the goddess Athena.

description_en: A series of interconnected subterranean spaces.
example: Derinkuyu in Turkey is a famous multi-level underground city.

meaning: sự phá hoại (công trình công cộng/của công)
description_en: Action involving deliberate destruction of or damage to public or private property.
example: The ancient statues were damaged during a period of religious vandalism.

description_en: A method of visually representing verbal communication.
example: The development of a writing system was a major leap for early human civilizations.

meaning: ngày xửa ngày xưa; năm ngoái
description_en: The recent past, especially a period of time that is different from now.
example: The movie evokes the romantic atmosphere of yesteryear.
